target.title: fix(tools): auto-correct hallucinated file extensions in tool call params (fixes #93326)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The model sometimes blends office-document extensions with "codex", producing paths like `report.docodex` instead of `report.docx`. This causes tool calls (read/write/edit) to fail with cryptic ENOENT errors, and in some runs the task summary may still report success despite failed file operations.
- Why now: This is a recurring issue affecting DOCX-related workflows. The previous approach (#93531) that rejected tool calls with suspicious extensions was too aggressive and was closed without merge.
- What changed: Auto-correct known hallucinated extension patterns (`.docodex`→`.docx`, `.pptcodex`→`.pptx`, `.xlscodex`→`.xlsx`, etc.) silently during tool parameter normalization.
- What did NOT change: Shell command validation (shell commands are free-form strings not amenable to pre-validation). Model prompting or inference behavior. Existing valid extension handling.
- Outcome: Tool calls with hallucinated extensions now succeed by auto-correcting to the intended extension instead of producing ENOENT failures.
- Reviewer focus: Whether the `correctHallucinatedFileExtension()` function correctly handles all known patterns, whether the normalization pipeline integration is clean, and whether false positives are sufficiently unlikely.

## Linked context

Closes #93326

Related #93531

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Tool calls with hallucinated extensions like `.docodex` now succeed by auto-correcting to the intended extension, instead of producing cryptic ENOENT failures -- #93326.
- Real environment tested: Node.js v22, local checkout on branch `fix/93326-hallucinated-file-extensions`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e "
import { correctHallucinatedFileExtension } from './src/agents/agent-tools.params.ts';

console.log('docodex ->', correctHallucinatedFileExtension('report.docodex'));
console.log('pptcodex ->', correctHallucinatedFileExtension('slides.pptcodex'));
console.log('xlscodex ->', correctHallucinatedFileExtension('data.xlscodex'));
console.log('docx ->', correctHallucinatedFileExtension('report.docx'));
console.log('txt ->', correctHallucinatedFileExtension('notes.txt'));
console.log('no ext ->', correctHallucinatedFileExtension('README'));
console.log('nested ->', correctHallucinatedFileExtension('path/to/file.docodex'));
"
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
docodex -> report.docx
pptcodex -> slides.pptx
xlscodex -> data.xlsx
docx -> report.docx
txt -> notes.txt
no ext -> README
nested -> path/to/file.docx
```

- Observed result after fix: All hallucinated extension patterns are auto-corrected to their intended forms. Valid extensions pass through unchanged. Directory path components are preserved.
- What was not tested: End-to-end behavior with actual LLM-generated tool calls in a full OpenClaw agent session. All possible hallucinated extension patterns (new patterns can be added as they are discovered).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses the real OpenClaw normalization pipeline but tests the correction function in isolation. It validates the extension mapping logic without depending on a live LLM or full agent session.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
pnpm vitest run src/agents/agent-tools.params.hallucinated-ext.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

```text
 ✓ src/agents/agent-tools.params.hallucinated-ext.test.ts (12 tests) 8ms
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > corrects .docodex to .docx
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > corrects .docxcodex to .docx
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > corrects .pptcodex to .pptx
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > corrects .xlscodex to .xlsx
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > preserves .docx unchanged
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > preserves .txt unchanged
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > preserves path with no extension
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > preserves directory components
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > handles case-insensitive matching
   ✓ correctHallucinatedFileExtension > handles empty input gracefully
   ✓ normalizeToolCallParams > integrates correction in normalization pipeline
   ✓ normalizeToolCallParams > does not modify non-hallucinated paths

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12)
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added 12 tests covering all known hallucinated patterns, valid extensions, edge cases (empty input, no extension, directory components, case sensitivity), and normalization pipeline integration.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Before this fix, tool calls with `.docodex` paths would fail with ENOENT errors because no file with that extension exists on disk.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The model sometimes conflates "codex" with office-document extensions when generating file paths, producing invalid extensions like `.docodex`. The tool parameter normalization pipeline had no awareness of these hallucinated patterns.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered valid extension handling but not hallucinated extension patterns. The normalization pipeline passed through invalid extensions without correction.
